name: Brute Force - Multiple Failed Logons
description: Detects repeated failed logon attempts against a Windows host, indicative of a brute-force credential attack. Triggers when 5 or more failed logon events (Event ID 4625) occur within a 2-minute window from the same source host.
author: SOC Lab
false_positives: Legitimate users mistyping their password repeatedly

name: Execution - Encoded PowerShell Command
description: Detects PowerShell being launched with an encoded command argument (-EncodedCommand or -enc). This technique is commonly used by attackers to obfuscate payload content.
query: event.code:"4688" and process.name:("powershell.exe" or "pwsh.exe") and process.command_line:(*EncodedCommand* or *-enc*)
author: SOC Lab

name: Reconnaissance - Network Port Scan Detected
description: Detects a high volume of network connection attempts in a short window, consistent with port scanning activity.
author: SOC Lab
false_positives: Vulnerability scanners run by IT teams

name: Persistence - New Service Installed
description: Detects installation of a new Windows service, which is a common persistence technique used by malware and attackers to survive reboots.
author: SOC Lab
